name: Apollo.io API
what_it_does: B2B sales intelligence and engagement platform API that provides contact and company search, enrichment, and automated email sequence execution from a database of 275M+ contacts.
use_cases: Searching for B2B contacts by job title, company, industry, or location for outbound prospecting agents
Enriching CRM records with phone numbers, email addresses, and firmographic data
Enrolling leads into automated email sequences triggered by agent workflows
Building targeted account lists based on technographic and firmographic filters
Syncing enriched contact data to Salesforce, HubSpot, or other CRMs via Apollo API
not_for: Consumer (B2C) contact data — database is entirely B2B professional profiles
Real-time phone number lookups with carrier-grade accuracy
Teams operating exclusively outside North America and Europe where data coverage thins
best_when: An agent needs to discover, enrich, and engage B2B prospects end-to-end within a single platform without stitching together separate data and email tools.
avoid_when: You only need one narrow capability (e.g., email verification only) where a cheaper point solution would suffice, or your contacts are primarily consumer/SMB.

auth.notes: API key passed as api_key in request body (POST) or as a query parameter. No fine-grained scopes — key has full account access. Key should be treated as a secret.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan includes 10,000 credits/month for email lookups and limited exports; sequence sending not included
pricing.paid_tiers: Basic $49/user/month, Professional $79/user/month, Organization $119/user/month (annual); credits for exports and enrichment scale with plan
pricing.notes: Credit system governs data export and enrichment. API access is included in paid plans; some endpoints (sequences, dialer) require higher tiers. Free plan has meaningful API limits.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by endpoint and plan; search endpoints typically limited to a few hundred requests/hour; enrichment is credit-gated rather than rate-limited

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include message strings but error codes are inconsistent across endpoints. Some failures return HTTP 200 with error payloads, which breaks standard error handling.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read endpoints (search, enrichment) are naturally idempotent. Sequence enrollment is not — re-enrolling a contact creates a duplicate entry unless checked beforehand.
known_agent_gotchas: Some error conditions return HTTP 200 with an error body — agents must inspect response payload, not just status code
Credits are consumed on enrichment even when the result is empty/not found; agents should pre-validate before bulk enrichment
Sequence enrollment has no built-in deduplication — agents must check existing enrollments before adding contacts
Contact export pagination uses page/per_page params with a max of 25 results per page — agents need explicit pagination loops
GDPR opt-out data is not reliably reflected in the API — requires separate compliance checks for European contacts

security_score_components.security_notes: API key in request body is a common but weaker pattern than header-based auth — logs and URL history may capture keys. No scopes means a leaked key grants full account access including sequence enrollment and data export.
